description: Joey, wearing a red baseball cap, looks forward and says, "Now. It's lodged my throat."
In a kitchen, Monica stands near a wooden table while Phoebe stands nearby with her back to the camera. Monica gestures with both hands as she speaks. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica looks at Phoebe and says, "Damn it. You know, this whole time we were concentrating on the babies and no one was watching." She moves her hands out to her sides.
Monica turns her head to look toward Chandler.
Monica says, "Chandler."
Chandler sits at the table, looking forward. He opens his mouth and sticks his tongue out. Loud audience laughter is audible.

The setting changes to a judge's office. Ross and Rachel stand before a large wooden desk where a judge sits in a black robe. A man sits at a stenograph machine. The judge says, "Okay. You two are asking the court for an annulment?" Rachel holds a red folder and responds, "Yes, Your Honor. And here are our forms, all filled out," as she hands the folder to the judge.

The judge looks through the papers in the folder and says, "So based on your petition, you are seeking an annulment on the grounds that Mr. Geller is mentally unstable." Rachel crosses her arms and looks toward Ross.
Rachel and Ross sit down in yellow chairs facing the desk. Ross looks briefly at Rachel and then back toward the judge.
Ross looks at the judge and says, "Fine. I'm mentally unstable." Rachel keeps her arms crossed.
The judge turns a page in the folder.
The judge continues reading, "And based on the fact that Mr. Geller is an intravenous drug user." Rachel looks straight ahead.
Ross turns his head quickly toward Rachel.
Ross says, "What?" His volume is higher than his previous line.
Rachel looks at the judge and says, "Yes. Heroin and crack." Ross keeps his gaze on Rachel.
Ross continues to stare at Rachel. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross turns to the judge and says, "Crack isn't even an intravenous drug." Rachel says, "Well, you would know," while looking toward the judge.
Ross stares at Rachel's profile while she looks forward.
The judge looks at the papers and says, "Now, it also says here that you lied about your sexual preference before marriage." Ross turns his head toward Rachel again.
Ross says, "Oh, come on," and gestures with his right hand toward Rachel. She says, "Ross, please. I found the magazine," without looking at him.
Ross looks back toward the judge.
The judge says, "And finally, that you were unable to consummate the marriage. Well, that makes sense since you're gay and addicted to heroin." Rachel raises her right hand to her mouth.
Ross presses his palms together in front of his chest.
Ross says, "Okay, I'm sorry. This is insane. I'm not addicted to heroin, I'm not gay. And there is no," with his pace faster than his previous lines. Rachel lowers her hand.

Ross continues, "problem with my ability to consummate anything. Look, I'll consummate this marriage right here, right now." He points his finger down toward the judge's desk. Rachel turns her head to look at him.
The judge looks up over her glasses and remains still.
The judge says, "That won't be necessary." Ross gestures with his right hand and says, "And when we were dating, we consummated like bunnies." Rachel lowers her gaze.
The judge taps a pen on the paperwork.

The judge says, "Now, if you two were involved in a serious relationship, that really creates a problem." Rachel points a finger and says, "Your Honor, rest assured, that relationship," interrupting the judge's sentence.
Rachel continues, "ended two years ago," while keeping her finger pointed toward the judge.
Rachel says, "And can you please strike consummated like bunnies? From the record?" The judge looks at the papers and asks, "Is there anything in this record that is actually true?"
Rachel looks toward Ross and then back to the judge. "Well, yes. We got married in Vegas. And the names, I think," Rachel says.
The judge says, "That's not the way the legal system works."
The judge continues, "This process is based on an objective review of the facts, not of the lie." She gestures with both hands.
Rachel raises her right hand and says, "Objection."
The judge looks toward Rachel and remains still.
The judge says, "It doesn't work like that either."
Ross says, "Overruled." Rachel turns to him and says, "Oh, stop it."
Ross and Rachel look back toward the judge.
The judge says, "Well, based on what I've heard, you two certainly don't qualify for an annulment." Ross and Rachel remain still.

The judge says, "If you don't want to be together, you'll have to file for divorce." Ross stands up and gestures toward Rachel. "That's great. You're happy now? Look what you did with your funny, funny form."
Rachel looks at Ross and says, "What? Me?"
Rachel stands up and says, "What about you and your consummated like bunnies nonsense?" while looking at Ross.
Ross turns his head away from Rachel and looks toward the stenographer.
Ross points at the stenograph machine and says, "What are you typing that for? She already said we don't get the annulment. Don't type that." The clicking of the machine is audible.
Ross walks toward the stenographer's desk.
Ross says, "Hey."
Ross says, "Stop typing."
Ross says, "Stop typing."
Ross says, "Stop typing," and reaches his hands toward the machine.
Ross pulls a long, continuous strip of paper out from the stenograph machine.
Rachel says, "Okay. Do you see?" gesturing with her hand toward Ross.
Rachel continues, "Do you see what you are keeping me married to?" The judge watches Ross.
The judge looks from Ross back toward Rachel.

The judge says, "You need to get out of my chambers." Rachel leans forward, placing both hands on the judge's desk, and says, "All right, look, lady, here is the deal. I came here for an annulment, and I am not."
Rachel continues, "leaving here until I get one." The judge looks at Rachel and asks, "Would you like to spend the night in jail?" Ross looks at Rachel.
Rachel stands up straight and says, "Thank you for your time." She turns and begins walking away from the desk.
Upbeat instrumental music begins. Ross picks up his jacket, puts it on, and follows Rachel out of the room.
The setting changes to a view of a multistory apartment building in a city. A yellow taxi is parked on the street in the foreground.
The setting changes back to the kitchen. Monica sits at the wooden table and picks up a small object from a bowl. Chandler sits next to her, looking toward her. The upbeat music continues.

Inside Monica’s apartment, the kitchen and living area are brightly lit. Monica and Chandler sit at a round wooden table, while Phoebe stands in the kitchen near a blue counter. Joey enters through the front door wearing a black and silver shiny jacket with a Porsche logo, a red cap with the same logo, and a red fanny pack. He places a large cardboard box labeled "Hats & Scarves" on the floor.
Joey walks toward the kitchen area while Monica and Chandler remain at the table. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey stands near the blue kitchen counter, looking toward Monica and Chandler. Audience laughter continues.
Joey gestures with his right hand toward his clothing and says, "So the Porsche guy took his car back."
Chandler holds a water bottle and touches his throat with his left hand as audience laughter is heard. Joey turns his head toward the kitchen.
Chandler keeps his hand on his throat and asks, "but you found the keys to his clothes?"
Audience laughter is audible. Joey shakes his head slightly while looking at Chandler.
Joey says, "No," his gaze remaining on Chandler.
Audience laughter continues as Joey turns toward Phoebe.

Joey gestures toward his chest and says, "I just love the way it feels when everybody thinks I own a Porsche." Monica, looking toward Joey, responds, "And people would think you own a Porsche because you're wearing the clothes."
Joey looks at Monica and says, "Of course," while raising his hands.
Audience laughter is audible as Joey pauses and looks toward Chandler.
Joey extends both arms to his sides and says, "only an idiot would wear this stuff if you didn't have a car."
Audience laughter continues. Joey looks down at his jacket while Chandler maintains his gaze on him.
Chandler says, "That is true," while keeping his left hand near his collarbone.
Audience laughter is heard. Phoebe stands behind the blue counter holding a spray bottle.
Phoebe looks toward Chandler and says, "Yeah, but only a genius would swallow a sonic blaster gun." Joey turns to look at her.
Chandler says, "I've been there," his volume lower than his previous line.
Audience laughter is audible as Joey pulls a pair of black gloves from his pocket and begins putting them on.
Joey looks around the room, adjusting his gloves, and says, "I am going to go drive my Porsche." He begins walking toward the door.

Monica says, "Joey, you know, you don't actually have one. Come on, what are you doing?" Joey turns back toward them at the door, gestures with his gloved hands, and says, "I'm in character. Will you talk to her?" He then opens the door and exits.
The door closes. Chandler remains seated, his left hand pressing against his throat.
Chandler says, "ah, I think it just moved. It's really poking me," his volume rising. Monica stands up and says, "Alright, that's it. We're going to the emergency room." Chandler stands up as well.
Phoebe, holding two baby bottles, looks toward them and says, "What? No, you can't. You can't leave me here with them."
Monica walks toward the living area and says, "We're babysitting. The babies are asleep. I'm sure you'll be okay on your own for a while." Phoebe responds, "But you can't leave me with them."
Phoebe raises the baby bottles and says, "We're a team. We're playing a zone. They're going to triple team me."
Audience laughter is heard. Chandler and Monica are standing near the living room furniture.
Monica says, "Something plastic lodged into his throat. We've got to go to the hospital." Phoebe shakes her head and says, "But no, because a doctor won't be able to help him."
Phoebe touches her temple with one bottle and says, "It's just going to naturally pass through his system in like seven years."
Audience laughter is audible. Chandler keeps his hand on his throat while looking at Phoebe.
Chandler says, "I think that's gum. I'm pretty sure it's gum."
Audience laughter continues. Monica stands near the door.
Chandler walks toward the door and says, "Okay, listen, this really hurts. Let's go," his vocal delivery strained.
Phoebe follows them and says, "But no, a real man wouldn't just run to the hospital. No."
Phoebe stands near the open doorway and says, "What would Krog do?"

Audience laughter is heard. The setting changes to an outdoor city street in the evening. Upbeat instrumental music begins. Joey leans against a brick wall under a blue "Bus Stop" sign, wearing his Porsche gear.
Joey looks down the street and says, "Why isn't that valet back with my Porsche?" A woman walks past him.
Audience laughter is audible. A bald man in a dark jacket walks past Joey and stops.
The man turns back to Joey, points at the keys in Joey's hand, and says, "Maybe because you've got the keys."
Audience laughter is heard as Joey looks down at the keys in his hand. More people walk past him on the sidewalk.
Rachel and Ross walk down the sidewalk together. Rachel says, "That's totally your fault."
Ross responds, "My fault? You threatened the judge." Rachel points ahead and says, "Well, you ripped the paper out of the court reporter's machine."
Ross gestures with his hands and says, "That was the only way I could get him to stop typing."
Ross and Rachel stop walking as they reach Joey, who is still leaning against the wall.
Joey looks at them and says, "Hi. How are the Gellers?" his vocal delivery higher in pitch.
Audience laughter is audible. Ross and Rachel look at Joey with flat facial expressions.
Rachel points a finger at Joey and says, "Don't call us that," her vocal delivery clipped and slow.
Audience laughter is heard. Ross looks at Joey.
Ross says, "The judge wouldn't let us get an annulment. Now we got to get a divorce."
Audience laughter is audible. Ross looks Joey up and down.
Ross says, "Did a Porsche throw up on you?" his vocal delivery tight.
Audience laughter continues as Ross and Rachel walk away from Joey. Joey remains leaning against the wall.
Joey calls out, "Hey, it's Porsche," gesturing toward his jacket.
Audience laughter is heard. Upbeat instrumental music plays. The setting changes back to Monica’s apartment. Phoebe is on the floor with three babies on a blanket.
Phoebe looks at the babies and says, "We ain't taking care of you with no, huh? You guys feel safe, right? Okay, I'm gonna take that spitbubble." She uses a cloth to wipe a baby’s face.
Phoebe looks at one of the babies and says, "Leslie? Yeah, I do."
Phoebe reaches down and lifts the legs of one of the babies.
Phoebe says, "Let's get rid of this dirty diaper. Leslie, I'll set you up with a nice clean one." She holds a bundled diaper.
Phoebe tosses the diaper toward a trash can, but it hits a small table nearby, knocking over a vase of flowers with a clatter. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe says, "Okay? I'm sorry. I didn't mean to scare you. I just have to clean that up, okay? Because let's face it, we're at Monica's." She begins to crawl toward the mess.
Audience laughter continues. Phoebe stands up and begins picking up the fallen flowers and the vase.
Phoebe says, "I broke it. All right, well, that's just the way things go. Okay, good." She places the flowers back onto the table.
Audience laughter is audible. Phoebe turns back toward the blanket where the babies are.
Phoebe looks at the blanket and asks, "Why are there only two of you?"
Audience laughter is heard. Phoebe looks around the floor.
Phoebe asks, "Where's Leslie?"
Phoebe looks at the two babies and says, "Well, you can't answer."
Phoebe begins searching under the sofa cushions and says, "Leslie, where are you? Leslie?"
Phoebe continues to look around the living room and says, "Leslie, now would be a good time for your first words."
Phoebe remains on the floor, looking toward the underside of the sofa. The babies stay on the blanket.
In a cluttered living room with chairs turned over and toys scattered, Phoebe leans forward toward a wooden dresser.
Phoebe looks into a half-open bottom drawer of the dresser where a baby is sitting. "Well, look at you," she says.
Audience laughter is audible as Phoebe reaches into the drawer.
Phoebe lifts the baby out of the drawer and holds the child up. "Hey, you are a little bit mischievous. My gosh," she says, her volume rising on "gosh." Audience laughter continues.
Baby gurgling and high-pitched vocalizations are audible.
Phoebe brings the baby closer to her face and says, "Oh, you're a lot mischievous." Audience laughter follows.
Phoebe walks toward a large fern on the dresser while holding the baby.
Phoebe looks at the fern and says, "It'll dry." Audience laughter follows as she uses her foot to kick the dresser drawer shut.
Phoebe walks away from the dresser, still holding the baby in both hands.
Phoebe lowers the baby onto a blanket on the floor and says, "You sit with your brother and sister now who aren't there." Audience laughter is audible.
Upbeat instrumental music plays. Outside, people walk past a brick apartment building with a red storefront and a dark blue awning at the corner of Bedford Street and Grove Street.

Inside the apartment, the living room and kitchen are in disarray. Chairs are overturned on the table, toys and papers are scattered across the floor, and a white refrigerator stands in the background.
Audience laughter plays over the visual of the messy room. Phoebe sits reclined in a tan armchair, holding a glass and a bottle.
The front door opens and Chandler enters, carrying a small white object and a paper bag. He stops and looks around the room.
Phoebe remains reclined in the chair and asks, "How do you feel?"

Chandler stands near the kitchen area, his hands in his pockets. "Well, let's just say that Krog will be fully equipped to destroy the universe again in twelve to 14 hours," he says. Audience laughter follows.
Chandler walks further into the room, stepping around a blue chair and a white playpen.
Phoebe raises both arms and says, "Okay, so I totally took care of the babies all by myself."
Phoebe continues, her hands moving as she speaks: "I fed them, bathed them and put them to bed and protected them from a tornado." Chandler's gaze stays on her. Audience laughter is audible.
The front door opens and Monica enters, carrying a purse, a bag, and mail. She stops at the entrance, looking at the room with her mouth slightly open.
Monica says, "Oh my God," with a brief pause between the words.
Phoebe looks toward Monica and says, "I know. The babies are asleep."
Monica walks toward the kitchen table, which is covered in objects and an overturned chair.
Monica gestures toward the room and asks, "What happened here?" Phoebe points to herself and says, "I did it. I took care of the babies all by myself." Audience laughter follows.
Monica keeps her arms extended, looking around at the clutter.
Chandler gestures toward Phoebe and says, "My apartment was the setting of Phoebe's triumph." Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe smiles and leans back in the armchair.
Chandler continues, "But the mess it's not as important as the fact that Phoebe took care of the babies all by." He stops mid-sentence as Monica turns her gaze toward him.
Monica and Chandler stand facing each other, Monica holding her bag and mail.
Monica lowers her hands and says, "Right, you're right."
Monica says, "I shouldn't freak out because this is what it's going to be like when you and I have babies. When will that be?" she asks, her volume rising on the question. Audience laughter follows.
Monica looks at Chandler. Chandler blinks and then slowly turns his head to look at the messy room.
Chandler points toward the living room and says, "Phoebe, would you take a look at this mess?" Audience laughter follows.
Upbeat instrumental music plays. Outside the apartment building, the streetlights are on and a red car is visible in the distance at night.
Inside another apartment, Rachel stands behind a table covered in boxes and papers. She is holding a white garment and looks toward the door.
Rachel says, "Oh, honey, thank God you're home. I was getting worried," her pace quick. Ross enters through the front door.
Ross enters carrying a leather briefcase. He closes the door behind him.
Ross walks into the room and says, "I picked up the divorce papers."
Ross opens his briefcase and pulls out a blue folder containing papers.

Ross says, "I've already signed everything. And I put little X's where you need to sign." Rachel sits on the sofa and says, "Oh, little x's. Great. That makes up for everything." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross stands in front of the sofa and begins to take off his navy blue jacket.
Ross says, "You know, you've done a lot of stupid stuff too," his volume louder than his previous line. Audience laughter follows.

Rachel asks, "Okay, name one stupid thing that is as stupid as this one." Ross responds, "Okay. How about you flew to London to stop my wedding? How about you told me..." Audience laughter overlaps his words.

Ross continues, "...you love me after I was already married?" Rachel stands and says, "Wait a minute. That was different. I did those things because I was in love with you." Ross says, "Yeah, right." Audience laughter follows.
Ross and Rachel stand facing each other in silence. Rachel looks away and then back toward Ross.
Ross looks down and then back at Rachel, saying, "You're right. That's very different." Audience laughter follows.
Rachel looks down at the folder on the coffee table.
Ross says, "So let's just sign the papers," his volume softer than before.
Rachel's eyes are lowered and her mouth is slightly open as she looks at the documents.
Ross looks at Rachel and asks, "What?"
Rachel looks up at him and says, "Nothing."
Rachel leans over the table and picks up a pen.
Ross says, "Okay, can we just sign, please?" His volume is low.
Rachel signs a paper. Ross reaches down, takes the paper, and moves it to the side to reveal another page underneath.
Rachel continues to sign the papers while Ross holds them steady for her. He looks at the documents as she writes.
Inside an apartment, Ross stands next to a table, holding a brown folder. Rachel sits on the opposite side of the table.
Ross holds the folder in one hand and a pen in the other. He looks toward Rachel and says, "Congratulations. Okay."
Rachel stands up from the table, moving toward the center of the room. "Wait, Ross. Wait a minute," she says.
Rachel stops and stands in front of Ross. She adjusts the cuffs of her white button-up shirt.
Rachel looks directly at Ross and says, "I kind of have a little confession." Her volume is softer than her previous line.
Ross shifts the folder in his hand and keeps his gaze on Rachel. The room is quiet except for ambient background noise.
Ross says, "What?" His volume is low.
Rachel looks at Ross and says, "Well, you know this whole marriage thing..." She raises her eyebrows slightly.
Rachel pauses and looks down toward her hands. Ross remains still.
Rachel looks back up and says, "...kind of my idea." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross continues to look at Rachel without moving. Laughter from the audience continues.
Ross says, "Excuse me?" Rachel continues, "Well, remember how we were too drunk to remember anything the night we were married?"
Ross says, "Yeah." Rachel asks, "And?" Ross says, "Yeah." Rachel continues her explanation.
Rachel says, "Yeah. I didn't want to say anything..." She clasps her hands together in front of her.
Rachel says, "...but it just kind of kept coming back to me." She gestures with both hands, palms facing herself.
Rachel says, "And remember we were in the casino..." She moves her hands apart and then back together.
Rachel says, "...and for some reason you thought it would be funny to eat a lot of grapes?" She raises her hands with palms out.
Ross blinks and remains standing still with the folder in his hand.
Rachel continues, "And I thought it would be funnier if we got married." Audience laughter becomes audible.
Rachel looks down at her fingernails and picks at one with her thumb.
Rachel says, "So as a compromise, we decided first to get married and then..." She pauses and gestures toward Ross.
Ross finishes the sentence, saying, "...to eat a lot of grapes." Rachel nods and says the same words at the same time. Laughter continues.
Laughter track remains audible. Rachel looks at her hands while Ross looks at the documents in his folder.
Rachel says, "Sorry I got us into this whole thing." She looks up at Ross, then shifts her gaze back down.
Ross looks at Rachel and says, "So then if you think about it, this is..."
Ross says, "...all your fault." His volume rises on "your fault." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel looks at Ross and keeps her hands clasped.
Rachel says, "Yeah. Don't push it, though." She keeps her gaze on Ross.
Ross looks down at the folder and starts to flip through the papers.
Ross says, "I got to say, I know we divorced a lot of women." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel crosses her arms over her chest and watches Ross as he continues to look at the papers.
Ross looks up at Rachel and says, "Never thought I'd be divorcing you." His volume is lower than his previous line.
Ross and Rachel look at each other in silence.
Rachel says, "I know." Her volume is soft.
Rachel looks down at the floor for a moment.
Rachel looks back at Ross and says, "I always thought if you and I got married that would be the one that stuck."
Rachel's gaze remains on Ross. He looks back at her while holding the folder.
Rachel says, "And it wouldn't be a secret." She shifts her stance slightly.
Ross looks up at the ceiling briefly before looking back at Rachel.
Rachel says, "And we wouldn't have our wedding dinner at Pizza Hut." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross laughs and rubs his right eye with his hand. Laughter track continues.
Ross says, "Did I even treat?" laughing while speaking. Rachel says, "No, it was on the house. It was a newlywed special."
Rachel touches the side of her neck with her hand. Audience laughter continues.
Ross says, "That may be the most depressing thing I've heard in my life." Laughter continues.
Ross looks down and closes the folder. Rachel keeps her hand on her neck.
Ross says, "I should probably get these to my lawyer's office." Rachel says, "Yeah."
Rachel says, "Hey, thanks, Ross, for taking care of all this." Ross says, "No problem."
Ross begins to walk toward the exit. Rachel steps forward and wraps her arms around Ross's torso. Ross hugs her back.
They remain in the hug. Rachel's head is on Ross's shoulder.
Ross pats Rachel's back twice with his hand, then they pull apart. Ross turns toward the door.
Rachel says, "I'm going to need a copy of those." Ross says, "Totally." He walks out of the room.
Upbeat vocal music begins. Outside on a sidewalk, Joey stands by a large object covered in a gray tarp. He wears a red cap and a blue-and-silver satin jacket.
A kid in a green jacket throws a football past the car cover. Joey gestures with both hands toward the kid.
Joey says, "Hey, careful around my Porsche." His volume is loud. He stands with his hands on his hips.
Joey remains by the car cover. Two women walk along the sidewalk toward him.
One of the women says, "Hi, Joey."
Joey smiles and says, "Hey, how you doing?"
The women stop walking and turn to face Joey. They look at the car cover.
One woman says, "He has the most amazing Porsche under there." She gestures toward the car cover.
Joey says, "I'd love to show you, but I just tucked her in. She's sleeping." Laughter is audible.
Joey looks at the women and smiles.
Joey says, "Hey, would you two girls like to go for a drink?" He gestures with his hand.
The kid in the green jacket runs across the sidewalk and falls onto the car cover. The object collapses into several cardboard boxes.
Joey and the women look at the pile of boxes. Laughter track continues. Music begins to play.
The screen is black. No sound is audible.
